✓ visibility.llm defaults to headless; tests asserting the visible path opt into pane; suite green: DEFAULT_CONFIG.visibility.llm flipped to "headless" in src/config/config.ts with template comment updated; config tests updated to assert headless default and overlay behavior; daemon pane-mode tests and consult driver-path tests now inject visibility: llm: pane; full zero-token suite verified green (213 passed, 1 skipped e2e).
✓ consult runs headless under the default and via the driver under visibility.llm: pane; both paths share the extractJson/fail-closed tail; a zero-token headless test proves no driver interaction: consult() branches on cfg.visibility.llm: headless path runs adapter.headlessCommand via sh() with stallMinutes*60_000 timeout, pane path is the prior driver code verbatim; both assign `out` and fall through to the shared extractJson + unparseable→human fail-closed tail; new test passes a driver whose slot/run/waitOutput/read/close all throw and asserts the verdict, proving zero driver interaction.
✓ With default config, judge/review/consult run headless (no visible panes) — the fleet the operator watches is workers only: Judge/review already gate on visibility.llm at the daemon level (headless when not pane); consult now gates the same way; with the new headless default no gate/consult call touches the driver, confirmed by the throwing-driver test and by pane-asserting tests now needing explicit llm: pane opt-in.
✓ consult honors visibility.llm: headless → plain headless call; pane → today's driver path verbatim: Headless branch is a plain sh() invocation of the adapter's headless command; pane branch preserves the exact prior slot/run/waitOutput(TICKMARKR_EXIT digit-guard)/read/close sequence including naming and keep semantics, unchanged.
✓ visibility.llm: pane still opts every gate/consult call back into a visible pane: Daemon pane-mode tests (named judge/review slots, keepPanes=run close-all) and consult driver-path tests pass with the explicit visibility: llm: pane overlay, exercising the visible-pane path end to end.
✓ The full zero-token suite stays green after the default flip (tests that assert the visible path opt into pane): npm test run in this worktree: 27 test files passed, 1 skipped (e2e); 213 tests passed, 1 skipped — green with the headless default in place.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- minor (non-blocking): headless consult concatenates stdout+stderr without a separator (llm.ts runHeadless inserts '\n'); harmless given extractJson's balanced-brace scan, but worth aligning
- minor (non-blocking): on sh() timeout the child is SIGKILLed and partial output flows to extractJson — this correctly fails closed to a 'human' verdict, matching the pane path's fail-closed tail

.planning/phases/03-starvation-visibility/03-03-SUMMARY.md
declared: .planning/phases/03-starvation-visibility/03-03-SUMMARY.md — created per the plan's<output> requirement, outside the declared file scope

✓ makeStub answers tab create (with root_pane) + pane split (success and non-zero variants); the grouped/orphan/ref-count/fallback tests are written and red; existing tests stay green.: makeStub gains opt-in tab (with root_pane) and pane-split cases including the exit-1 variant; 4 new tests exist in a dedicated RED commit (17f4043) before the feat commit; full suite runs 216 passed / 1 skipped.
✗ Grouped worker slots share one ref-counted tab, stack full-width via downward split, reap the orphan root pane, degrade cleanly on split failure, and shq every arg; no-group and subprocess paths unchanged.: The happy path is correct, but the degrade path is not clean: the fallback member reaches `if (group && tabId) this.groups.set(group, {tabId, lastPaneId: id, refcount: 1})`, which CLOBBERS the existing group entry — losing splitUnsupported and the real refcount. Subsequent close() of the first member then decrements the wrong entry and, at refcount 0, issues `tab close` on the fallback member's tab while that agent may still be running.
✓ The worker caller passes its role group; judge/review/consult grouping deferred; the full suite is green and the build is clean.: daemon.ts worker slot() call passes "workers"; judge/review/consult call sites untouched; npm test 216 passed and tsc build clean (verified in the worktree).
✓ Concurrent same-group (worker) slots share ONE herdr tab; the second+ member stacks full-width via a downward pane split, never a per-slot tab: Second+ member issues `pane split <lastPaneId> --direction down --no-focus`, joins the group tab, and the test asserts exactly one `tab create` across two members.
✗ The group tab is created exactly once (first member) and torn down only when its last member closes (ref-counted pane close, then tab close at refcount 0): Holds only while splits succeed. After a split failure the fallback member's unconditional groups.set() resets refcount to 1 for a group with 2 live members and swaps in the fallback tab id, so the original group tab is never tab-closed via refcount and `tab close` fires while a member is still alive. The stub masks this by returning identical tab ids for every `tab create`.
✓ A tab-hosted slot leaves NO orphan empty root shell pane beside the working agent (the auto-created root pane is reaped): root_pane.pane_id is parsed from `tab create` and reaped via `pane close <rootPaneId>` when distinct from the agent pane; test asserts `pane close w1:p0`.
✓ A slot with no group keeps today's per-slot tab behavior — but still reaps its orphan root pane: The no-group call takes the pre-existing per-slot tab path unchanged plus the new root-pane reap; the orphan-reap test exercises exactly this (slot with no group).
✗ If pane split is unavailable or errors, the grouped slot degrades to today's per-slot tab — no regression: The degraded slot itself gets a per-slot tab, but the fallback introduces a regression: it overwrites the group's GroupState (refcount=1, splitUnsupported dropped, tabId replaced). With split failing and two live workers, whichever grouped member closes first triggers `tab close` on the OTHER member's tab, killing a live agent — worse than the pre-change tab-per-slot behavior. splitUnsupported being dropped also means every later member retries the failing split. The fallback test passes only because the stub returns the same tab_id (w1:t1) for both `tab create` calls, so the wrong-tab close is indistinguishable.
✓ Every interpolated arg (group label, pane ids, root pane id) passes through shq(): All shell-interpolated ids go through shq (pane split lastPaneId, agent rename paneId/name, pane close rootPaneId/slot.id, tab close tabId); the group label is used only as a Map key, never interpolated into a command.

data.summary: Implemented ref-counted role-tab grouping with orphanroot-pane reap in HerdrDriver, fixing the prior attempt's fallback-clobber bug, with RED-then-GREEN testsand full suite/build green.

✓ Stub answers tab create (with root_pane) + pane split (success and non-zero variants); grouped/orphan/ref-count/fallback tests written; existing tests stay green: Diff adds makeCapableStub answering tab create with tab_id+root_pane, agent start with counter-distinct pane ids, and pane split with both a success variant (fresh pane id) and a splitFails exit-1 variant. All four tests (grouped split, orphan reap, ref-counted close, split-failure fallback) are written. The original makeStub is untouched, so existing tests keep their prior behavior (no root_pane → no reap call → old assertions hold). The criterion names makeStub as the vehicle, but the required stub capability and test coverage are fully delivered via the added stub without regressing existing tests.
✓ Concurrent same-group (worker) slots share ONE herdr tab; second+ member stacks full-width via a downward pane split, never a per-slot tab: slot(cwd,name,group) checks this.groups; a live group routes through trySplit('pane split <lastPaneId> --direction down --no-focus') and returns a Slot carrying the group's tabId. Test verifies exactly one 'tab create', the downward split, no '--direction right', and s2.tabId === s1.tabId.
✓ Group tab created exactly once (first member) and torn down only when last member closes (ref-counted pane close, then tab close at refcount 0): First member seeds GroupState with refcount 1; each split member increments. close() on a grouped slot does 'pane close <pane>', decrements, and only issues 'tab close <tabId>' + group delete at refcount ≤ 0. Test asserts no 'tab close' after closing the first member and 'tab close <tabId>' after the last.
✓ A tab-hosted slot leaves NO orphan empty root shell pane beside the working agent (root pane reaped): newTabSlot now parses root_pane.pane_id from tab create and issues 'pane close <rootPaneId>' when it differs from the agent's pane id. This runs for both grouped first members and no-group slots; the orphan test verifies 'pane close w1:p01' and that the reaped pane isn't the agent pane.
✓ A slot with no group keeps today's per-slot tab behavior — but still reaps its orphan root pane: No group → straight to newTabSlot, identical tab-per-slot flow plus the root-pane reap; slot.group stays undefined so close() takes the existing tab-close path. Covered by the 'no-group slot reaps its tab's orphan root pane' test.
✓ If pane split is unavailable or errors, the grouped slot degrades to today's per-slot tab — no regression: trySplit returns undefined on non-zero exit or unparseable/missing pane id; the group is marked splitUnsupported and the member falls back to newTabSlot. Critically, the fallback does NOT re-seed the live group (guarded by !this.groups.has(group)) and returns a group-less slot, so the fallback's own tab close can't kill a sibling and the real group tab still ref-counts to zero. The splitFails test verifies own-tab fallback, two tab creates, and that closing the surviving group member tears down the group's tab, not the fallback's.
✓ Every interpolated arg (group label, pane ids, root pane id) passes through shq(): shq wraps g.lastPaneId in the split, paneId+name in agent rename, rootPaneId in the reap, the resolved pane in the ref-counted close, and g.tabId in the tab close. The group label is used only as an in-memory Map key and is never interpolated into a shell command, trivially satisfying the requirement.
✓ The worker caller passes its role group; judge/review/consult grouping deferred; suite green and build clean: daemon.ts passes "workers" as the third slot() arg for workers only; no changes to gates/llm.ts or run/consult.ts (deferred as required). The ExecutorDriver interface makes group optional, so SubprocessDriver's two-arg slot() remains assignable and the subprocess path is untouched. Tracing each new test against the implementation and the capable stub (log-count-based ids, quote-stripped $@ logging) shows every assertion satisfied, and existing tests are unaffected by the additive changes.

reviewer cursor-agent:composer-2.5 (cursor): requested changes
- Concurrent grouped workers get different worktrees (daemon passes per-task `wt` to `slot()`), but the split path never sets cwd in the new pane — only `newTabSlot` does via `agent start ... --cwd`. A second+ member inherits the first pane's shell cwd, so with default concurrency 3 two live workers can edit the wrong worktree.
- The split join path only does `pane split` + `agent rename`; it skips the `agent start ... --cwd ... -- bash` bootstrap that solo/first members use. Research A1 (rename makes a split shell addressable for `agent get` / `wait agent-status`) is unverified and not covered by tests — interactive grouped workers may fail status/wait semantics even if cwd were fixed.
- `lastPaneId` is never updated when the closed member was the most recent split target; closing the newest pane then opening another member splits a dead pane, sets `splitUnsupported`, and permanently degrades the group while older members remain in the shared tab.
- Task-1 acceptance asked to extend `makeStub`; implementation adds separate `makeCapableStub` instead (minor spec drift).
- Orphan root-pane reap is only asserted for no-group slots; grouped first-member reap is implemented but untested.
- Split-failure fallback test checks tab count and `group` omission but does not assert the planned `agent start ... --tab` fallback command sequence for the degraded member.

data.notes: This is not a task problem — cursor-agent is hard-stuck on its interactive 'Workspace Trust Required' prompt in the fresh worktree (each tickmarkr worktree is a new untrusted path), so the worker never even started; retrying the same CLI will stall again unless trust is pre-granted (e.g. cursor-agent flag/config to auto-trust, worth a harness fix later). Reroute back to claude-code (sonnet or opus), whose previous attempt passed build/test/scope/acceptance and only failed cross-vendor review. Feed it the reviewer's concrete findings as the work list: (1) split-path panes never set cwd — second+ group members inherit the first pane's shell cwd and can edit the wrong worktree; run the agent bootstrap with --cwd (or equivalent) in the split pane, not just rename; (2) verify/cover that a renamed split pane actually supports agent get / wait agent-status semantics; (3) update lastPaneId on close so closing the newest member doesn't make the next split target a dead pane and permanently degrade the group; (4) add the missing tests: grouped first-member root-pane reap and the degraded member's fallback command sequence.

data.summary: Grouped worker slots now share one ref-counted full-widthherdr role tab with orphan root-pane reaping, per-member cwd + verified rename on split joins, live-membersplit-source tracking, and memoized per-slot-tab fallback — all review findings fixed, 220-test suite andbuild green.

✓ makeStub answers tab create (with root_pane) + pane split (success and non-zero variants); grouped/orphan/ref-count/fallback tests written; existing tests stay green: makeStub gains StubOpts {tab, splitFails, renameFails}: tab create echoes tab_id w1:t1 + root_pane w1:p0 (opt-in, default '{}' preserves old callers), pane split echoes result.pane.pane_id or exits 1. Tests A/B/C/C2/D + rename-failure cover grouped/orphan/ref-count/fallback. Verified live: full suite 219 passed, 1 skipped — existing tests green.
✓ Concurrent same-group (worker) slots share ONE herdr tab; second+ stacks full-width via downward split, never a per-slot tab: groupSlot: first member creates the tab, later members joinGroup via `pane split <src> --direction down --no-focus`. Test A runs two slot() calls under Promise.all and asserts exactly one `tab create`, split off the live source pane, and shared tabId w1:t1; asserts no `--direction right`.
✓ Group tab created exactly once (first member) and torn down only when last member closes (ref-counted pane close, then tab close at refcount 0): groups Map + groupSerial serializes concurrent slot()/close() so two first members can't both create the tab. closeGrouped does pane close per member, filters members, and issues `tab close` + Map delete only at length 0. Test C asserts no `tab close` after first close and exactly one after the last.
✓ A tab-hosted slot leaves NO orphan empty root shell pane beside the working agent (auto-created root pane reaped): tabSlot captures result.root_pane.pane_id and issues `pane close <rootPane>` when it differs from the agent pane. Tests A and B both assert `pane close w1:p0`.
✓ A slot with no group keeps today's per-slot tab behavior — but still reaps its orphan root pane: slot() without group goes straight to tabSlot (per-slot tab, tab close on close() unchanged) which now also reaps the root pane. Test B asserts per-slot tab create/close plus the w1:p0 reap and group undefined.
✓ If pane split is unavailable or errors, the grouped slot degrades to today's per-slot tab — no regression: joinGroup returns null on split non-zero, unparseable pane id, rename failure (with best-effort reap of the split pane), or failed cd; caller memoizes splitUnsupported and falls back to tabSlot with group stripped. Tests D (split fails, memoized after one try, full agent-start bootstrap per degraded member) and the rename-failure test cover both paths.
✓ Every interpolated arg (group label, pane ids, root pane id) passes through shq(): All new interpolations are shq-wrapped: pane close (root pane, split pane, member pane), pane split source, agent rename pane+name, pane run pane + cd command (cwd shq'd twice, inner+outer), tab close tabId. The group label is only a Map key and never reaches a shell string.
✓ The worker caller passes its role group; judge/review/consult grouping deferred; the full suite is green and the build is clean: daemon.ts worker slot() gains the third arg "workers"; no judge/review/consult call sites changed (deferred as specified). Verified live in the worktree: `npm run build` (tsc) clean, `npm test` 219 passed / 1 skipped.

reviewer cursor-agent:composer-2.5 (cursor): approved
- Group label is never interpolated into any herdr command: first-in-group tab create still uses `tab create --label <slot name>` (tabSlot) instead of `shq(group)`, so shared role tabs remain labeled with opaque per-worker slot names rather than "workers" — partial miss of D-07 fleet-readability intent (03-RESEARCH:218), though the plan explicitly kept the name-based tab-create path.
- Joined group members are bootstrapped via pane split + agent rename + pane run cd only (no `agent start -- bash`); interactive blocked/idle paging depends on `agent get`/`agent_status` working on rename-born shell panes — research A1 is unverified live, with D-09 covering split/rename failure but not a silent A1 miss at runtime (deferred to Phase-4 dogfood per plan).
